metadata.title: Product Experiences
metadata.description: Outcome-based product experiences for the Ride Sharing Marketplace domain, covering rider trips, driver earnings, business travel, and fleet operations.

definitions.productBrick: A reusable implementation-facing building block used to realize one or more product experiences.
definitions.productCapability: A high-level, outcome-based experience that delivers customer or business value.

metadata.flowModelNote: Each experience carries only experience-specific flows and names the supporting bricks used in each step.

capabilities.name: Book and Complete a Reliable Trip
capabilities.description: Help a rider move from intent to completed trip with predictable pickup, travel, payment, and support.
outcomes: Higher trip conversion
Better repeat rider retention

flows.title: Book and Complete a Reliable Trip
flows.description: Help a rider move from intent to completed trip with predictable pickup, travel, payment, and support.

steps.title: Enter trip request
keyFacts: This step determines whether the rider sees the right set of products and prices.
A weak request flow amplifies both abandonment and support later.
painPoints: Riders abandon when pricing feels opaque or product choice is confusing.
Address and pickup ambiguity creates later dispatch friction.

dependencies.name: Trip Request and Intent Capture
dependencies.description: Captures rider pickup, destination, timing, ride type, account context, and trip intent before dispatch begins.
dependencies.how: Uses Trip Request and Intent Capture to create a clean demand signal.

dependencies.name: Dynamic Pricing and Incentives
dependencies.description: Calculates upfront fares, surge multipliers, discounts, and driver incentives.
dependencies.how: Uses Dynamic Pricing and Incentives to present credible upfront pricing.

dependencies.name: Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience
dependencies.description: Composes request-flow state, offers, and server-driven actions for rider app surfaces.
dependencies.how: Uses Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience to change rider request surfaces and actions without coupling every marketplace change to a full mobile release.

steps.title: Match and track driver
keyFacts: Match quality drives both rider trust and driver efficiency.
Pickup execution matters as much as raw ETA.
painPoints: Repeated rematches or cancellations erode confidence quickly.
Airport and venue pickups become brittle without stronger guidance.

dependencies.name: Dispatch and Matching Engine
dependencies.description: Matches rider demand to the best available driver or fleet supply.
dependencies.how: Uses Dispatch and Matching Engine to allocate the best supply.

dependencies.name: ETA, Maps and Routing Intelligence
dependencies.description: Provides route estimation, pickup guidance, airport geofencing, and live map context.
dependencies.how: Uses ETA, Maps and Routing Intelligence to keep pickup and arrival expectations credible.

dependencies.name: Safety and Trust Operations
dependencies.description: Provides identity assurance, trip safety tooling, trust controls, and incident workflows.
dependencies.how: Uses Safety and Trust Operations to provide identity and safety confidence throughout the trip.

steps.title: Complete and pay
keyFacts: Closing the trip cleanly affects both next-trip intent and support cost.
Receipts and issue handling matter for rider trust.
painPoints: Unexpected charges or weak support create long-lasting trust damage.
Family and business contexts increase billing complexity.

dependencies.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
dependencies.description: Manages rider identity, payment methods, receipts, refunds, and billing context.
dependencies.how: Uses Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments to settle the trip and deliver receipts.

dependencies.name: Payments Platform and Ledger
dependencies.description: Provides checkout orchestration, payment execution, and auditable money movement.
dependencies.how: Uses Payments Platform and Ledger to authorize, capture, and reconcile trip payments reliably across payment methods.

dependencies.name: Safety and Trust Operations
dependencies.description: Provides incident workflows and trip-support controls.
dependencies.how: Uses Safety and Trust Operations to handle issues when the trip did not go as expected.

capabilities.name: Turn Time Online Into Quality Earnings
capabilities.description: Help drivers stay productive, eligible, and confident in how marketplace work turns into income.
outcomes: Higher supply retention
Better supply liquidity at peak demand

flows.title: Turn Time Online Into Quality Earnings
flows.description: Help drivers stay productive, eligible, and confident in how marketplace work turns into income.

steps.title: Stay eligible to drive
keyFacts: Supply falls away when compliance feels random or punitive.
Fast document and status handling improves driver trust.
painPoints: Blocked accounts or stale documents create avoidable churn.
Drivers lose confidence when they do not understand status changes.

dependencies.name: Driver Onboarding and Compliance
dependencies.description: Supports driver signup, screening, document verification, and eligibility management.
dependencies.how: Uses Driver Onboarding and Compliance to keep drivers eligible and informed.

steps.title: Receive worthwhile trip offers
keyFacts: Dispatch quality and pricing legibility jointly shape supply behavior.
Peak-hour trust is built through actual productivity, not only heatmap visuals.
painPoints: Bad pickup geometry and weak rematch logic waste active time.
Opaque incentives create supply skepticism.

dependencies.name: Dispatch and Matching Engine
dependencies.description: Matches supply with demand while honoring geography and market rules.
dependencies.how: Uses Dispatch and Matching Engine to create productive supply allocation.

dependencies.name: Dynamic Pricing and Incentives
dependencies.description: Calculates pricing and incentives based on marketplace conditions.
dependencies.how: Uses Dynamic Pricing and Incentives to expose worthwhile opportunities.

steps.title: Understand payouts and incentives
keyFacts: Drivers stay longer when earnings are explainable.
Payout timing and adjustment transparency affect trust materially.
painPoints: Hidden adjustments reduce driver confidence.
Weak payout visibility creates support load.

dependencies.name: Driver Earnings and Incentive Management
dependencies.description: Calculates and explains driver earnings, incentives, adjustments, and payouts.
dependencies.how: Uses Driver Earnings and Incentive Management to make earnings understandable and actionable.

dependencies.name: Payments Platform and Ledger
dependencies.description: Provides payout execution and financial ledger integrity.
dependencies.how: Uses Payments Platform and Ledger to execute disbursements and preserve audit-safe payout consistency.

capabilities.name: Run a Business Mobility Program
capabilities.description: Help a business admin manage rider convenience, policy compliance, and spend visibility from one program.
outcomes: Higher managed travel adoption
Better spend control and retention

flows.title: Run a Business Mobility Program
flows.description: Help a business admin manage rider convenience, policy compliance, and spend visibility from one program.

steps.title: Configure travel policy
keyFacts: Admins need control without creating employee adoption friction.
Policy and billing logic must stay aligned.
painPoints: Disconnected policy and billing systems create manual work.
Rigid controls lower traveler adoption.

dependencies.name: Business and Fleet Controls
dependencies.description: Manages business travel policies, fleet accounts, supplier setup, and reporting access.
dependencies.how: Uses Business and Fleet Controls to set policy and account structure.

dependencies.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
dependencies.description: Manages identity, payment methods, receipts, and billing context.
dependencies.how: Uses Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments to apply payment and receipt rules.

dependencies.name: Payments Platform and Ledger
dependencies.description: Provides checkout and financial orchestration across payment methods and markets.
dependencies.how: Uses Payments Platform and Ledger to execute compliant checkout, reconciliation, and spend controls.

steps.title: Review program performance
keyFacts: Reporting quality determines whether finance trusts the program.
Admins need cost, compliance, and adoption in one view.
painPoints: Fragmented reporting reduces trust and slows renewals.
Weak insight into out-of-policy behavior prevents improvement.

dependencies.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
dependencies.description: Provides KPI models, marketplace health monitoring, experimentation feedback, and business reporting.
dependencies.how: Uses Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics to report on spend, policy, and traveler usage.

capabilities.name: Resolve Trip Issues Through Self-Service and Assisted Support
capabilities.description: Help riders and drivers resolve trip, payment, and trust issues quickly through structured self-service, AI-assisted triage, and agent-assisted workflows.
outcomes: Faster issue resolution
Lower support cost without hurting trust

flows.title: Resolve Trip Issues Through Self-Service and Assisted Support
flows.description: Help riders and drivers resolve trip, payment, and trust issues quickly through structured self-service, AI-assisted triage, and agent-assisted workflows.

steps.title: Submit a structured issue
keyFacts: Structured issue intake reduces back-and-forth and improves routing quality.
Trip context and issue taxonomy determine whether self-service can resolve the case.
painPoints: Free-form support creates delays and inconsistent routing.
Users lose trust when they cannot find the relevant trip context quickly.

dependencies.name: Support Automation and Issue Resolution
dependencies.description: Captures issue type, runs self-service flows, and routes support work.
dependencies.how: Uses Support Automation and Issue Resolution to structure intake and determine the next-best resolution path.

dependencies.name: Trip Request and Intent Capture
dependencies.description: Stores trip context and lifecycle state.
dependencies.how: Uses Trip Request and Intent Capture to attach the issue to the right trip and operational history.

steps.title: Resolve financial or trust outcome
keyFacts: Financial and trust issues require the right combination of automation and escalation.
Resolution quality shapes retention more than raw ticket closure speed.
painPoints: Billing corrections feel opaque when receipts, adjustments, and trust flows are disconnected.
Serious incidents escalate poorly when support and trust systems are split.

dependencies.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
dependencies.description: Manages receipts, refunds, and billing context.
dependencies.how: Uses Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments to execute receipts, credits, refunds, and billing corrections.

dependencies.name: Safety and Trust Operations
dependencies.description: Provides incident handling and trust workflows.
dependencies.how: Uses Safety and Trust Operations when the issue requires escalation, investigation, or sensitive trust handling.

steps.title: Triage and assist resolution
keyFacts: AI-assisted support only works when grounded in trip, payment, policy, and trust context.
Agents need suggested actions, explanations, and guardrails rather than unbounded automation.
painPoints: Generic chat automation creates inconsistent answers and weak policy adherence.
Support teams cannot improve automation when resolution outcomes and issue taxonomies are disconnected from analytics.

dependencies.name: Experimentation and ML Decisioning Platform
dependencies.description: Provides AI-assisted triage, model governance, and decisioning support.
dependencies.how: Uses Experimentation and ML Decisioning Platform to recommend next-best support actions, evaluate automation quality, and apply model governance to customer-impacting support flows.

dependencies.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
dependencies.description: Publishes support metrics, issue trends, and resolution feedback loops.
dependencies.how: Uses Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics to measure deflection quality, resolution fairness, and recurring issue patterns.

capabilities.name: Detect and Stop Fraud With Low Customer Friction
capabilities.description: Help the marketplace prevent payment abuse, account takeover, collusion, and chargeback exposure while keeping legitimate riders and drivers moving with explainable, proportionate friction.
outcomes: Lower fraud losses
Lower false-positive friction for legitimate users

flows.title: Detect and Stop Fraud With Low Customer Friction
flows.description: Help the marketplace prevent payment abuse, account takeover, collusion, and chargeback exposure while minimizing friction for legitimate users.

steps.title: Score risk at key checkpoints
keyFacts: Fraud prevention has to act in fractions of a second at sensitive checkpoints.
The same user can look different across payment, account, and marketplace behavior.
Chargeback signals and payment anomalies are most useful when they feed back into risk models quickly.
painPoints: Slow risk checks damage conversion and trip completion.
Weak checkpoint coverage leaves expensive abuse paths open.

dependencies.name: Fraud and Risk Decisioning
dependencies.description: Runs real-time rules and graph-based fraud scoring.
dependencies.how: Uses Fraud and Risk Decisioning to score abuse risk and return a low-latency decision.

dependencies.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
dependencies.description: Provides account and payment context for risk checks.
dependencies.how: Uses Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments to evaluate account and billing patterns at checkout and account events.

dependencies.name: Payments Platform and Ledger
dependencies.description: Provides transaction, chargeback, refund, and ledger context for financial risk.
dependencies.how: Uses Payments Platform and Ledger to incorporate early chargeback signals and payment-account anomalies into risk decisions.

steps.title: Act with proportionate friction
keyFacts: The best fraud response is often a step-up check, not a blanket block.
Operational trust teams need explainable actions and escalation paths.
Explainable model signals help tune challenge thresholds without masking customer harm.
painPoints: Overblocking hurts legitimate riders and drivers.
Poor escalation paths leave trust teams reacting too late.

dependencies.name: Safety and Trust Operations
dependencies.description: Provides trust workflows, escalations, and incident operations.
dependencies.how: Uses Safety and Trust Operations to operationalize risk actions and handle escalations.

dependencies.name: Driver Onboarding and Compliance
dependencies.description: Provides eligibility and supply identity context.
dependencies.how: Uses Driver Onboarding and Compliance when supply-side identity and eligibility checks must be enforced.

dependencies.name: Experimentation and ML Decisioning Platform
dependencies.description: Provides model explainability, experiment measurement, and governed ML decisioning.
dependencies.how: Uses Experimentation and ML Decisioning Platform to evaluate risk challenge impact, explain model behavior, and tune friction against fraud loss and false-positive metrics.

capabilities.name: Launch and Tune Marketplace Changes With Controlled Experiments
capabilities.description: Help marketplace teams prototype, roll out, evaluate, and tune pricing, dispatch, support, AI, and growth changes safely with experiment assignment, holdouts, ML predictions, responsible-AI controls, and measurement feedback.
outcomes: Faster product iteration with lower rollout and model risk
Better marketplace decisions through measurable treatment effects

flows.title: Launch and Tune Marketplace Changes With Controlled Experiments
flows.description: Help marketplace teams prototype, roll out, evaluate, and tune pricing, dispatch, support, AI, and growth changes safely with experiment assignment and measurement.

steps.title: Assign treatments and decision logic
keyFacts: Rollout control matters as much as the underlying decision model.
Dispatch and pricing need both online scoring and explicit guardrails.
AI-enabled changes need reviewable prototypes and responsible-AI controls before they affect customer workflows.
painPoints: Direct launches create regressions that are hard to attribute.
Inconsistent assignment and feature logic weakens experiment quality.

dependencies.name: Experimentation and ML Decisioning Platform
dependencies.description: Provides treatment assignment, feature serving, and model prediction support.
dependencies.how: Uses Experimentation and ML Decisioning Platform to assign treatments and serve reusable decision logic.

dependencies.name: Dynamic Pricing and Incentives
dependencies.description: Consumes experiments for price and incentive behavior.
dependencies.how: Uses Dynamic Pricing and Incentives as a controlled decision surface for pricing treatments.

dependencies.name: Dispatch and Matching Engine
dependencies.description: Consumes experiments for allocation and matching behavior.
dependencies.how: Uses Dispatch and Matching Engine as a controlled decision surface for dispatch policy changes.

dependencies.name: Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience
dependencies.description: Provides server-driven experience contracts and AI-assisted product prototyping.
dependencies.how: Uses Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience to prototype customer-facing changes and ship composed experience treatments without forcing every backend change into an app release.

steps.title: Measure and tune outcomes
keyFacts: Holdouts and shared metrics prevent teams from overfitting to local wins.
Fast measurement cycles increase iteration speed only if metrics stay trustworthy.
Model and GenAI launches need evaluation signals beyond business lift, including safety, quality, and adherence.
painPoints: Weak observability hides regressions in rider, driver, or trust outcomes.
Disconnected metrics make experiment decisions slow and political.

dependencies.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
dependencies.description: Provides aggregated metrics and marketplace feedback loops.
dependencies.how: Uses Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics to measure impact, compare treatments, and tune rollout decisions.

dependencies.name: Data Platform and Streaming Foundation
dependencies.description: Provides ingestion, data-lake, and shared data-processing foundations.
dependencies.how: Uses Data Platform and Streaming Foundation to keep experiment and model data fresh, consistent, and reusable at scale.

dependencies.name: Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation
dependencies.description: Provides rollout safety, benchmarking, and runtime observability.
dependencies.how: Uses Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation to catch performance regressions, benchmark shared services, and roll changes forward or back safely.

capabilities.name: Run Reliable Rider and Driver Mobile App Experiences
capabilities.description: Help the marketplace deliver fast, synchronized, observable, and consistently instrumented mobile experiences for riders and drivers across network conditions and app surfaces.
outcomes: Lower app friction on critical trip flows
Faster diagnosis of broken or degraded mobile experiences
More trustworthy cross-platform mobile analytics

flows.title: Run Reliable Rider and Driver Mobile App Experiences
flows.description: Help the marketplace deliver fast, synchronized, observable mobile experiences for riders and drivers across network conditions.

steps.title: Keep apps synchronized in real time
keyFacts: Pickup, route, and support flows degrade quickly when app state lags backend state.
Realtime messaging and failover behavior are part of the product experience, not only infrastructure.
Shared mobile analytics contracts are needed to compare rider and driver funnels across iOS, Android, and web-adjacent surfaces.
painPoints: Polling or weak connection management creates stale rider and driver state.
Network outages are customer-visible if failover is not handled in the app stack.

dependencies.name: Mobile App Experience Platform
dependencies.description: Provides realtime messaging, mobile networking failover, and shared app instrumentation.
dependencies.how: Uses Mobile App Experience Platform to keep rider and driver apps synchronized and resilient.

dependencies.name: Trip Request and Intent Capture
dependencies.description: Provides trip state and lifecycle context.
dependencies.how: Uses Trip Request and Intent Capture as the underlying trip state that must be reflected cleanly in the app.

dependencies.name: Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience
dependencies.description: Composes mobile-facing state, view models, and action contracts.
dependencies.how: Uses Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience to keep mobile surfaces thin while still adapting quickly to changing trip, support, and special-flow requirements.

dependencies.name: Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation
dependencies.description: Provides edge traffic control, failover, and observability foundations.
dependencies.how: Uses Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation to preserve app continuity when traffic patterns or runtime conditions change.

steps.title: Instrument and diagnose mobile flows
keyFacts: Mobile reliability cannot be separated from analytics quality and client-to-edge observability.
Generated funnel definitions and shared event schemas reduce instrumentation drift across platforms.
painPoints: Different mobile platforms often report subtly different events for the same customer journey.
Client networking failures are hard to debug when the mobile-to-edge hop is not observable.

dependencies.name: Mobile App Experience Platform
dependencies.description: Provides mobile analytics, performance measurement, and networking observability.
dependencies.how: Uses Mobile App Experience Platform to standardize funnel instrumentation, detect client regressions, and expose mobile-to-edge diagnostics.

dependencies.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
dependencies.description: Publishes product and operational metrics from mobile events.
dependencies.how: Uses Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics to compare mobile funnel health across rider and driver experiences.

capabilities.name: Move Money With Compliant Checkout and Payout Control
capabilities.description: Help the marketplace collect, safeguard, reconcile, and disburse money reliably across rider checkout, refunds, and driver payouts.
outcomes: Higher checkout conversion with lower financial failure rates
Stronger financial control and auditability

flows.title: Move Money With Compliant Checkout and Payout Control
flows.description: Help the marketplace collect, safeguard, reconcile, and disburse money reliably across rider checkout and driver payouts.

steps.title: Orchestrate checkout and funds movement
keyFacts: Checkout logic spans authorization, risk checks, payment-method specifics, and asynchronous confirmation.
The same platform needs to serve collections and disbursements without losing auditability.
Hot payment accounts and immutable account changelogs need throughput and correctness controls.
painPoints: Payment logic duplicated across flows slows launches and creates inconsistent support.
Asynchronous processors and local methods complicate correctness and recovery.

dependencies.name: Payments Platform and Ledger
dependencies.description: Provides checkout orchestration, ledgering, and money movement.
dependencies.how: Uses Payments Platform and Ledger to unify checkout, accounting, and payout execution.

dependencies.name: Fraud and Risk Decisioning
dependencies.description: Provides low-latency payment risk decisions.
dependencies.how: Uses Fraud and Risk Decisioning to protect payment and payout flows without excessive user friction.

steps.title: Audit and reconcile financial state
keyFacts: Ledger correctness depends on automated reconciliation, accounting data tests, and recoverable processing.
Financial analytics and risk feedback loops need transaction data that is timely and audit-safe.
painPoints: Manual audit sampling cannot keep up with internet-scale financial transaction volume.
Payment support and fraud teams lose trust when ledger, receipt, and risk views disagree.

dependencies.name: Payments Platform and Ledger
dependencies.description: Provides ledger, reconciliation, audit, and high-throughput payment-account processing.
dependencies.how: Uses Payments Platform and Ledger to validate account state, reconcile money movement, and preserve an auditable transaction history.

dependencies.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
dependencies.description: Publishes financial quality metrics and operational reporting.
dependencies.how: Uses Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics to expose financial correctness, failure, chargeback, and reconciliation metrics.

capabilities.name: Feed Analytics and ML With Fresh Governed Data
capabilities.description: Help the marketplace keep experimentation, analytics, search, recommendations, and ML systems supplied with fresh, large-scale, low-latency, and governable data across marketplace and financial workflows.
outcomes: Faster experimentation and model iteration
Higher trust in shared analytical and ML data
Lower-latency search, recommendation, and operational analytics

flows.title: Feed Analytics and ML With Fresh Governed Data
flows.description: Help the marketplace keep experimentation, analytics, search, recommendations, and ML systems supplied with fresh, large-scale, and governable data.

steps.title: Ingest and govern shared data
keyFacts: Freshness, scale, and governance are tightly linked in a scaled marketplace analytical ecosystem.
Many downstream capabilities fail quietly when ingestion and metadata quality slip.
Streaming ingestion, data replication, and federated metadata must be treated as product foundations for ML and analytics.
painPoints: Batch-only pipelines slow decision loops for experimentation, analytics, and ML.
Monolithic metadata and weak ownership create shared-fate outages and poor trust.

dependencies.name: Data Platform and Streaming Foundation
dependencies.description: Provides ingestion, storage, and shared data-processing foundations.
dependencies.how: Uses Data Platform and Streaming Foundation to ingest, store, and govern shared analytical data at scale.

dependencies.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
dependencies.description: Publishes business and operational metrics.
dependencies.how: Uses Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics to turn governed data into business and operator signal.

steps.title: Serve search, recommendation, and analytics workloads
keyFacts: Marketplace discovery and decisioning increasingly need OLAP, search, vector retrieval, and embedding-based recommendation patterns.
Data lake observability and cost controls are needed when petabyte-scale data platforms become operational dependencies.
painPoints: Batch-only data access cannot support low-latency recommendation, search, or operational intelligence workloads.
Unobserved data lake I/O and stale indexes create expensive regressions that look like product-quality problems.

dependencies.name: Data Platform and Streaming Foundation
dependencies.description: Provides OLAP, search/vector serving, data replication, and data lake observability.
dependencies.how: Uses Data Platform and Streaming Foundation to serve low-latency analytical queries, maintain search/vector indexes, and monitor data platform health.

dependencies.name: Experimentation and ML Decisioning Platform
dependencies.description: Provides model serving, recommendation decisioning, and governed AI workflows.
dependencies.how: Uses Experimentation and ML Decisioning Platform to consume fresh features, serve embeddings and recommendations, and evaluate model quality.

dependencies.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
dependencies.description: Provides search/recommendation intelligence and metric governance.
dependencies.how: Uses Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics to connect discovery, ranking, and marketplace KPIs into shared operational metrics.

capabilities.name: Keep Marketplace Services Resilient Under Global Load
capabilities.description: Help the marketplace keep core services available and responsive under global load, overload events, edge or runtime failures, and large-scale platform changes.
outcomes: Lower incident impact on rider and driver flows
Safer traffic management and operational response
Lower regression risk from shared platform and codebase-wide changes

flows.title: Keep Marketplace Services Resilient Under Global Load
flows.description: Help the marketplace keep core services available and responsive under global load, overload events, runtime failures, and large-scale platform changes.

steps.title: Protect runtime and route around failures
keyFacts: Overload protection and failover are product-facing when trips, payouts, and support rely on them.
Traffic control is more valuable when it can be changed without redeploying business services.
Benchmarking and shift-left tests prevent shared runtime changes from quietly degrading marketplace services.
painPoints: Fragmented throttling and weak failover increase blast radius during incidents.
Without strong observability, teams cannot distinguish bad product changes from runtime failure.

dependencies.name: Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation
dependencies.description: Provides traffic control, failover, and observability foundations.
dependencies.how: Uses Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation to protect shared services and steer traffic safely under stress.

dependencies.name: Mobile App Experience Platform
dependencies.description: Provides mobile-side failover and realtime experience reliability.
dependencies.how: Uses Mobile App Experience Platform to preserve customer-visible continuity when the network path changes.

steps.title: Verify platform change safety
keyFacts: Shared platform migrations need automated tests, benchmarks, and progressive rollout controls.
Developer productivity work becomes product-facing when test instability or regressions slow marketplace teams.
painPoints: Large migrations create hidden risk when service owners must hand-review every change.
Performance regressions are expensive when discovered only after traffic shifts.

dependencies.name: Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation
dependencies.description: Provides test migration automation, adaptive benchmarking, rollout control, and observability.
dependencies.how: Uses Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation to validate platform changes, detect regressions, and reduce codebase-wide migration risk.

dependencies.name: Data Platform and Streaming Foundation
dependencies.description: Provides platform telemetry, data lake observability, and operational data feeds.
dependencies.how: Uses Data Platform and Streaming Foundation to feed benchmark, incident, and platform-health signals into reliability analysis.
